title: New Zealand activity index  | Stats NZ
content:
## New Zealand activity index
26 May 2026, 10:45am
The New Zealand activity index increased 2.1 percent on April 2025. Grid demand, electronic card transaction activity, heavy traffic movements, and job advertisements all recorded increases. In contrast, the performance of manufacturing index, activity outlook, and light traffic movements decreased, while jobseeker numbers recorded a fall.
New Zealand activity index, annual percentage change, September 2003–April 2026
New Zealand activity index, annual percentage change, September 2003–April 2026
NZAC summarises several monthly indicators, including spending, unemployment, job vacancies, traffic volumes, electricity generation, business outlook, and manufacturing activity.
NZAC is a broad measure of economic activity. It provides government policymakers, markets, and the general public with a more timely and frequent indicator of economic activity, which complements a more definitive measure, such as GDP (gross domestic product).
